title: Roldana heteroidea
taxonomy: Roldana heteroidea is classified within the family Asteraceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. This classification places Roldana heteroidea in the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants that also includes other families such as Senecioneae and Cichorieae. The genus Roldana is typically placed within the subfamily Asteroideae, which is one of the largest and most widespread subfamilies within Asteraceae. Roldana heteroidea is often referred to as a member of the tribe Senecioneae, a group of daisies and related plants that are characterized by their small to medium-sized flower heads. By placing Roldana heteroidea within this classification, botanists can better understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of this plant.

intro: Roldana heteroidea, also known as Mexican Roldana, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Roldana heteroidea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Roldana heteroidea is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and respiratory issues.
